items.title: Jonas
items.content:
Jonas

Race: Human | Class: Fighter (Eldritch Knight) | Level: 10

Who He Is

Jonas is a cocky warrior who genuinely enjoys violence. He's good at fighting and he knows it — and he's not shy about letting others know it too. His brash confidence often clashes with the cosmic forces the party keeps stumbling into, but that doesn't stop him from swinging first and asking questions later.

The Bleeding Blade

Jonas carries a cursed longsword called The Bleeding Blade — a weapon that hungers. It must be fed blood daily or it turns on its wielder. The sword whispers to him sometimes. He's learned to manage the curse, but managing isn't the same as controlling.

Marked Twice

Jonas bears two divine marks:

  • Azerach's Brand — The Whispered King marked him during their passage through the Silent Dominion. The brand burns when secrets are spoken nearby. Through this brand, Azerach opened the Closed Locket and revealed Vor'Kael's true name.
  • Talaris's Attention — After using fire in the Blooming Rot, the Verdant King took notice. The forest remembers what he burned.

Vor'Kael's True Name

Jonas holds Vor'Kael's true name — the name she had before the Usurpation, kept secret by Azerach for 800 years. He acquired it from a Closed Locket purchased in Lastwell, which responded to Azerach's brand on his hand. This is ultimate leverage against the Hollow Empress.

The cost: Jonas spoke her true name aloud twice in her domain. Vor'Kael will hunt him forever for this transgression.

Key Moments

  • Hired alongside Sol as escorts to Veinspire (Session 0)
  • Survived the 13-Hour Trial in the Clockwork Sanctum
  • Claimed the Bleeding Blade from the Altar after killing Savaros
  • Watched Kael die in the Catacomb Reliquary
  • Traveled through the Blooming Rot (Fursen carried Mira's remains in a barrel)
  • Lied to the Choir Blooms and was marked for it
  • Stabbed Fursen twice after being betrayed (then reconciled)
  • Acquired Vor'Kael's true name from the Closed Locket
  • Saved Kael's Echo by catching the Codex at the Mouth of the World
  • Traversed the Throat of Forgotten Things

Current Status

Jonas is on an empty road, 2 days from Veinspire. He holds Vor'Kael's true name — ultimate leverage against a Sovereign, but also a death sentence. The Hollow Empress will hunt him forever. His relationship with Fursen is damaged but mending after their reconciliation.

items.title: Sol Raven
items.content:
Sol Raven

Race: Skeleton (God-Woken) | Class: Sorcerer | Level: 10

Who He Is

Sol Raven is not alive in the traditional sense. He is a skeleton — bare bones animated by a shard of divine essence from Aedwynn the Maker, the god who was murdered to create the Sovereigns. He wears robes and a hood to hide his nature from the living.

He has no memory of who he was before he woke up as bones. He doesn't know how he died, why Aedwynn saved him, or if the thoughts in his skull are truly his own.

The Maker's Witness

In Session 18, after the party killed Kaleth, they found a hidden chamber called the Chamber of the Final Breath. A white-barked tree with veins of gold rose from the stone, and at its base sat Aedwynn's preserved body — a shell containing an echo of her consciousness. When the party touched her, she awakened. Sol was recognized as carrying a piece of the Maker, and he accepted the role of Witness — the Maker's heir, carrying her memory forward.

He can now access complete memories of the world before the Usurpation, share memories through touch, and create permanent objects from nothing. Divine abilities from a source that predates the Sovereigns.

Clockmaker's Touch

During the 13-Hour Trial, Sol traded a personal memory for a temporal ability called Clockmaker's Touch. He's still learning what it can do.

Infected with Possibility

In the Concept Graveyard within Vor'Kael's Throat, Sol opened the Door of Possibility. He saw every version of himself he could have been — Sol with flesh and breath, older Sol surrounded by grandchildren, Sol as a respected mage, a thousand Sols who asked "Why did you become THIS instead of US?"

Fursen pulled him back and the door slammed shut, but the Possibilities poured in. Sol is now infected with unlived lives:

  • Disadvantage on Wisdom saves until purged or accepted
  • Once per day, can ask "What would have happened if...?" about a past decision and receive a true answer
  • Whispers of unlived lives grow louder in moments of doubt or regret
  • Unknown long-term effects

In the presence of the Satiated One, the whispers went silent — the first silence since the infection.

The Remembrance Seal

Sol retrieved a Remembrance Seal from a skeleton in Vor'Kael's bile pools — a woman who almost escaped 800 years ago, dissolving inches from freedom. The black iron pendant grants advantage on saves against memory alteration and contains her final memory.

Key Moments

  • Hired as escort to Veinspire with Jonas (Session 0)
  • Sensed divine resonance in the corrupted church catacombs
  • Ascended for the first time in a floating village on a river of blood
  • Traded a memory for Clockmaker's Touch during the 13-Hour Trial
  • Touched Aedwynn's body and became the Witness
  • Showed Talaris Bloomrend a vision of the Maker alive
  • Placed Mira's wedding ring on her finger in the Cave of Waiting
  • Retrieved the Remembrance Seal from the Throat's bile pools
  • Opened the Door of Possibility and became infected with unlived lives
  • Nearly lost Kael's Echo when the Codex tried to throw itself into Vor'Kael's Mouth

Current Status

Sol is on an empty road, 2 days from Veinspire. He carries the Resonant Codex (containing Kael's Echo — nearly lost at the Mouth of the World), the Remembrance Seal, and an infection of Possibility that whispers of all the lives he never lived. The weight of being the only living connection to a murdered god has grown heavier.

items.title: Fursen
items.content:
Fursen

Race: Leonin | Class: Monk (Open Hand) | Level: 10

Who He Is

Fursen is a Leonin monk — a lion-like humanoid who follows the Way of the Open Hand. He joined the party after they freed him from temporal imprisonment during the 13-Hour Trial. He is one of the last surviving Leonin — a species that was exterminated over a thousand years ago.

The Temporal Prison

Fursen was trapped in one of Imhuran's temporal cells, frozen outside of time. The party's actions during the 13-Hour Trial freed him — but they also learned that four other Leonin remain trapped in similar cells somewhere.

The 10,000 Names

Fursen's blood carries the memory of 10,000 Leonin ancestors. At the Mouth of the World — the vast depression where Vor'Kael waits — his ancestors' names screamed in recognition. In the presence of the Satiated One, they fell silent for the first time, quiet before something so ancient.

The Ledger of Moments

When the party visited Thessaly at the Frayed Codex in Veinspire, Fursen begged for The Ledger of Moments — a heavy tome bound in clockwork with tiny gears still turning in the cover. Thessaly gave it to him for free, with a promise:

"Come back someday. Tell me how your quest concludes."

The Ledger contains a partial transcription of Imhuran's temporal records — names, crimes, sentences. It updates itself: new names appear, old names vanish. Most importantly, it contains Leonin history, including information about Fursen's mother Vaelith.

Sekris's Contract-Token

In Lastwell, after Fursen betrayed Jonas (giving his name to the identity-stealing husks) and Jonas retaliated (throwing the Bleeding Blade into Fursen's thigh), a meaning-dealer named Sekris approached Fursen with a smoke-grey coin.

Breaking the coin summons Sekris to arrange for meanings to be "removed." The implication: Fursen could arrange for Jonas's death. The deal is not yet made — Fursen holds the token but hasn't broken it.

Key Moments

  • Freed from temporal imprisonment during the 13-Hour Trial (Session 8)
  • Gave a pale lifespan coin to the Misremembered (Session 8A)
  • Received the Ledger of Moments from Thessaly (Session 18)
  • Witnessed Kael and Mira's deaths in the Catacomb Reliquary
  • Carried Mira's remains in a barrel through the Blooming Rot
  • Completed the quest to reunite Mira with Elias
  • Betrayed Jonas in Lastwell by giving his name to the husks
  • Stabbed twice by Jonas (Bleeding Blade + Booming Blade)
  • Received contract-token from Sekris the meaning-dealer
  • Pulled Sol back from the Door of Possibility
  • Reconciled with Jonas (accepted medical kit and 100 gold)

Current Status

Fursen is wounded but healing — thigh and shoulder wounds from Jonas's blades. He carries the Ledger of Moments, the weight of 10,000 ancestors in his blood, and Sekris's contract-token (unused). His relationship with Jonas is damaged but mending. The Satiated One spoke to him of the choice between avenging his people or finding stillness.

items.title: Kael
items.content:
Kael

Role: Former Companion | Status: Deceased (Session 18)

Who He Was

Kael was a Resonant Theorist — a scholar who studied the connections between the Sovereigns and the fragments of the murdered god. He joined the party in Veinspire and traveled with them through multiple domains.

The Impossible Boy

During the 13-Hour Trial, the party learned the truth: Kael was never born. He was a paradox — a being who existed despite having no origin point. He emerged from the cracks between moments, sustained by borrowed time and stolen meaning.

This revelation broke him. He spent days questioning whether he was real, whether his memories meant anything, whether he deserved to exist.

Sol told him: "It doesn't matter. Because you're here. Right now."

His End

In the Catacomb Reliquary (Session 18), the party faced Savaros — a Skin-Walker serving Ultharion who had killed and replaced the previous High Keeper. Savaros consumed both Mira Alethain and Kael.

But Kael killed Savaros from the inside. His impossible nature destroyed the creature's ability to process meaning. They found Kael's skeleton beside the Resonant Codex, which had transformed — now containing an echo of who he was.

Burial

The party buried Kael behind the Church of the Merciful Vein. Sol spoke at his grave: "He was real because he chose to be."

info.description: The Thirteen who murdered a god

items.title: Mareatha the Vein-Mother
items.content:
Mareatha the Vein-Mother

Domain: Veinspire (Sangreal Dominion) | Divine Organ: The Circulatory Engine

What She Is

Mareatha claimed the Heart of Aedwynn the Maker during the Usurpation. She is blood incarnate - a living circulatory system that spans her entire domain. Every river, canal, and stream in the Sangreal Dominion flows with divine blood. The city of Veinspire is built where her heart beats loudest.

Her Domain

The Sangreal Dominion is a land of blood. Arterial rivers flow between settlements. Citizens drink filtered blood-water. The Church of the Merciful Vein is one of many religious institutions, and the Tribunal handles various matters throughout the domain.

Mareatha has not been seen for some time. Rumors say she sleeps, though none know why. When she was active, she spoke through her blood - through the canals, through communion wine, through the veins of her faithful. Her Inquisition roots out those who would challenge her divine right, even in her absence.

Party Encounters

The party spent Sessions 0-4 in Mareatha's domain. They explored Veinspire, descended into corrupted church catacombs, and eventually fled through underground tunnels when the Tribunal closed in.

They never met Mareatha directly, but they felt her presence in every drop of blood, every crimson canal, every whispered prayer.

items.title: Azerach the Whispered King
items.content:
Azerach the Whispered King

Domain: Dominion of Still Air | Divine Organ: The Maker's Lungs

What He Is

Azerach claimed the Lungs of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. He hears everything. Every whispered secret, every unspoken thought, every confession made in the dark - they all reach him eventually.

He collects forbidden knowledge the way others collect gold. Truths that would shatter minds, secrets that would end dynasties, confessions that would damn souls - all catalogued in the libraries of silence.

His Domain

The Dominion of Still Air is a place of enforced quiet. Sound is dangerous here - speak too loudly and Azerach might hear. Speak a secret and he definitely will. The inhabitants communicate through sign language and written notes, saving their voices for prayers and emergencies.

Party Encounters

The party traveled through the Dominion of Still Air in Sessions 6-7. They experienced the Bell of Breath - a massive construct that marks the boundary between silence and sound. They learned to move quietly, speak carefully, and guard their thoughts.

Azerach branded Jonas during their passage. Jonas had thrown his hammer at a massive silent bell in Azerach's realm — though it made no sound, Azerach felt it and found Jonas amusing. The mark burns when secrets are spoken nearby. It is not punishment, but curiosity — Azerach has marked him for observation.

items.title: Imhuran the Regent of Hours
items.content:
Imhuran the Regent of Hours

Domain: The Clockwork Sanctum | Divine Organ: The Heart of Time

What He Is

Imhuran claimed the Heart of Time during the Usurpation. He sees time as others see space - past, present, and future laid out before him like a map. He judges the worth of moments, deciding which deserve to exist and which should be pruned from the timeline.

He is obsessed with efficiency. Wasted time is sin. Procrastination is heresy. Every second must serve a purpose or it will be removed.

His Domain

The Clockwork Sanctum is a realm of gears and pendulums, where time flows differently in different chambers. Some rooms age you decades in minutes. Others preserve you forever in a single frozen moment. The Regent's servants move on precise schedules, their lives measured to the second.

Party Encounters

The party underwent the 13-Hour Trial in Sessions 7-8. This was a test of temporal endurance - thirteen hours compressed and stretched through impossible spaces. During the trial:

  • Sol traded a memory for Clockmaker's Touch
  • Kael's paradox nature was revealed
  • Fursen was freed from temporal imprisonment
  • The party proved their moments had worth

items.title: Eredain the Choirless Monarch
items.content:
Eredain the Choirless Monarch

Domain: Elystra (The Frozen Reaches) | Divine Organ: The Resonance Organ

What He Is

Eredain claimed the Resonance Organ of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. He controls gravity, sound, emotion, and resonance. When he speaks, reality listens. His hymns can crush mountains or lift cities into the sky.

He was once a composer, creating music for the Maker. Now he rules a realm where emotion affects gravity and temperature - grief freezes, joy thaws.

His Domain

Elystra defies normal physics. Frozen crystalline formations dominate the landscape. Sound becomes tangible - spoken words can form into objects, music can reshape terrain. Choir Priests serve as Eredain's enforcers, their hymns carrying the weight of divine law.

Party Encounters

In Session 5, the party encountered a Choir Priest after stealing a hymn fragment from one of Eredain's acolytes. The confrontation led to the priest's death, a collapsing church, and the party's flight through underground tunnels into a river of blood.

Sol ascended for the first time in a floating village - one of the first signs of his connection to Aedwynn.

items.title: Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King
items.content:
Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King

Domain: The Blooming Rot (Talar'Noth) | Divine Organ: The Liver (Regeneration Core)

What He Is

Talaris claimed the Liver and regenerative organs of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. He controls the cycle of decay and rebirth - things rot quickly in his domain, but they also grow back stronger. Death feeds life. Life feeds death. The cycle never stops.

Before the Usurpation, he was a physician who watched countless patients die. He studied regeneration, seeking the secret to conquering death. What he found was different: death isn't the enemy. Death is part of the system.

His Domain

The Blooming Rot is a forest that thinks. Every plant is connected through fungal networks. Every death feeds new growth. Pilgrims travel established paths, and those who stray rarely return unchanged.

The Verdant King doesn't rule from a throne - he IS his domain. His consciousness spreads through every root and spore. When the forest watches you, Talaris is watching.

Party Encounters

The party traveled through the Blooming Rot in Sessions 18-19 to reach Verdant Pass. They passed through Sporehaven, the Sweetwater Marsh, Pilgrim's Spine, the Hunger Garden, and the Congregation Grove.

They burned his creatures with fire - twice - which angered the forest. But at the Congregation Grove, Sol shared a memory of Aedwynn the Maker with Talaris. The Verdant King saw his creator alive for the first time since the Usurpation.

The Choir Blooms wept golden spores. Talaris said: "She was... beautiful."

The fire is not forgiven. But the memory changed something.
items.raw: Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King Domain: The Blooming Rot (Talar'Noth) | Divine Organ: The Liver (Regeneration Core) What He Is Talaris claimed the Liver and regenerative organs of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. He controls the cycle of decay and rebirth - things rot quickly in his domain, but they also grow back stronger. Death feeds life. Life feeds death. The cycle never stops. Before the Usurpation, he was a physician who watched countless patients die. He studied regeneration, seeking the secret to conquering death. What he found was different: death isn't the enemy. Death is part of the system. His Domain The Blooming Rot is a forest that thinks. Every plant is connected through fungal networks. Every death feeds new growth. Pilgrims travel established paths, and those who stray rarely return unchanged. The Verdant King doesn't rule from a throne - he IS his domain. His consciousness spreads through every root and spore. When the forest watches you, Talaris is watching. Party Encounters The party traveled through the Blooming Rot in Sessions 18-19 to reach Verdant Pass. They passed through Sporehaven, the Sweetwater Marsh, Pilgrim's Spine, the Hunger Garden, and the Congregation Grove. They burned his creatures with fire - twice - which angered the forest. But at the Congregation Grove, Sol shared a memory of Aedwynn the Maker with Talaris . The Verdant King saw his creator alive for the first time since the Usurpation. The Choir Blooms wept golden spores. Talaris said: "She was... beautiful." The fire is not forgiven. But the memory changed something.

items.title: Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress
items.content:
Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress

Domain: Vor'Khalem (The Devoured Courts) | Divine Organ: The Digestive Engine

What She Is

Vor'Kael claimed the Stomach and Bowels of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. She consumes meaning itself - concepts, identities, memories, purposes. Everything she devours becomes part of her, digested into something new.

She is eternally hungry. No amount of consumption satisfies her. She has eaten entire kingdoms, swallowed the identities of thousands, and still the emptiness remains.

Her Domain

Vor'Khalem is a realm of consumption. Digestive tunnels, acid marshes, regurgitated landscapes. Things lose meaning as they approach her throne. Names become sounds. Purposes become habits. Identities become echoes. Those who serve her longest eventually forget why they're serving at all.

Connection to Kael

Kael - the party's former companion - was vomited from Vor'Kael's domain. He was a paradox, a being with no origin point who existed despite never being born. The Hollow Empress may have been trying to consume him, or he may have escaped from somewhere inside her. He never knew which.

In the Catacomb Reliquary, when Savaros consumed Kael, his impossible nature destroyed the creature from within. Perhaps there was something of Vor'Kael in him after all - something that couldn't be digested.

items.title: Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd
items.content:
Ultharion the Obsidian Shepherd

Domain: Umbrasseur Isles (The Blackfold) | Divine Organ: The Skin and Boundary Membrane

What He Is

Ultharion claimed the Skin of Aedwynn during the Usurpation. He controls form and appearance - the boundary between self and world. His servants, the Skin-Walkers, can wear the faces of others, stealing identities and memories.

His Domain

The Umbrasseur Isles are shadow-haunted islands with mirror seas and skin cliffs. Identity fractures here. Reflections are independent. Shadows hunt.

Connection to Savaros

Savaros the High Keeper — the creature who killed Mira and Kael in the Catacomb Reliquary — was one of Ultharion's Skin-Walkers. He had killed the previous High Keeper and stolen his identity to infiltrate the Church of the Merciful Vein.

When Kael destroyed Savaros, the Obsidian Shepherd lost one of his servants. He knows. He's already asking about Leonin records.

But the party does not know. Not yet.

The Corpse-Continent

The world existed before Aedwynn's death, but when the Maker was slain, the land conformed to the shape of her corpse. The Thirteen Sovereigns now rule domains built from her stolen organs.

The Domains

  • Ossathar (North-Central) — Vortegas the Bone-Warden
  • Dominion of Still Air (West) — Azerach the Whispered King

  • The Blooming Rot (East-Central) — Talaris Bloomrend

items.title: Veinspire
items.content:
Veinspire

Domain: The Sangreal Dominion (Mareatha) | Type: Major City

Overview

Veinspire is the beating heart of Mareatha's domain — a city built where the Vein-Mother's stolen divine heart pumps loudest. The city follows a ring-district design, with multiple walled districts surrounding a central cathedral. Arterial canals flow through every district. Buildings are stained red with centuries of blood-mist. The walls literally breathe.

The Tribunal operates throughout the city, handling many matters beyond just religious enforcement. The Church of the Merciful Vein is one of many churches in the city, though the Grand Cathedral dominates the skyline.

Districts

The Lesser Sacral District

A quieter district with lower divine presence and moderate Inquisition activity. Key locations include:

  • The Frayed Codex — A narrow bookshop run by Thessaly, an impossibly old bookseller. Officially sells hymnals and devotional texts. Actually deals in restricted texts, pre-Usurpation fragments, and forbidden knowledge.
  • The Whetstone and Anvil — A smithy run by Garrett, a discreet craftsman who doesn't ask questions and doesn't talk to the Church.
  • Church of the Merciful Vein — A small congregation church, station of High Inquisitor Seraphine Vale.
  • The Catacomb Reliquary — West side of the district, main Tribunal checkpoint. Where prisoners go to the deep cells. Was operated by Savaros the High Keeper.

The Dregs

Veinspire's poorest district. Ramshackle buildings, market stalls, sluggish canals. No one looks at you directly here — seeing things gets you involved. Unlike the rest of the city, the buildings don't pulse or breathe. No divine presence reaches this far.

The Grand Cathedral

The central religious structure where citizens receive communion (filtered blood-water).

The Catacombs

Beneath the city lies an extensive catacomb network that connects to older tunnels and eventually leads to the Catacomb Reliquary.

Party History

First Visit (Sessions 0-4)

The party arrived in Veinspire as escorts for a merchant caravan. They explored the city and descended into the catacombs.

They descended into the catacombs, witnessed Sister Marwen's corruption by divine tendrils, and were interrogated by the clergy. High Inquisitor Seraphine Vale declared them "potential heretics" and forbade them from leaving. The party met Kael, a young scholar with resistance connections, and discovered the Resonance Theorists — a hidden cell studying divine memory.

They infiltrated the church archives and stole the Resonant Codex. Kael joined the party. They eventually escaped through the southern mines and fled to the Pallid Crossroads as fugitives.

Return Visit (Sessions 17-18)

The party returned via the Arterial Tunnels beneath the city, emerging in the Lesser Sacral District. They visited:

  • The Frayed Codex — Thessaly recognized Mira from 30 years ago. Sol read The Doctrine of Still Air and a book on Vor'Kael that has a mind of its own, but returned them — too expensive. Fursen begged for The Ledger of Moments and Thessaly gave it to him for free, with a promise to return and tell her how his quest concludes.
  • The Whetstone and Anvil — Garrett assessed Jonas's Custodian Armplate and shared legends about lost artifacts.
  • Church of the Merciful Vein — Confronted Seraphine Vale. Mira revealed she had delivered Seraphine at birth 34 years ago. The party showed Seraphine evidence of Kaleth Vorn's crimes. Seraphine's faith cracked and she cooperated, providing intelligence on Savaros, the Cardinal, and the Confluence.
  • The Catacomb Reliquary — Descended to confront Savaros the High Keeper. Both Mira and Kael died in the battle. Savaros was destroyed when Kael's paradox nature proved indigestible.

The party escaped through the Dregs with Savaros's Ledger — 15 centuries of Tribunal intelligence.

Current Status

The Confluence has occurred. While the party was in the Blooming Rot laying Mira to rest, the Tribunal completed their ritual. The party doesn't know what happened or what changed. Seraphine's status is unknown.

items.title: The Clockwork Sanctum
items.content:
The Clockwork Sanctum

Domain: Imhuran the Regent of Hours | Type: Temporal Realm

Overview

The Clockwork Sanctum is a realm where time is tangible. Mountain-sized gears turn through different time periods. Hourglass monoliths contain compressed decades. Rivers of liquid chronology flow through fractured courtrooms that replay trials forever. Prisons hold inmates who age a century per day. Caverns exist where time reverses with each breath.

Everything serves a schedule. The Regent's servants move on precise timetables, their lives measured to the second. Citizens experience different time rates based on social class. Waste is heresy. Inefficiency is sin.

The 13-Hour Trial (Sessions 7-8)

The party was pulled into the Clockwork Sanctum and forced to endure a divine sentencing machine — thirteen hours compressed and stretched through impossible temporal spaces.

The Trials

  • Hours I-III: The Arterial Collapse (testing temporal pressure), the Infinite Loop (a corridor repeated endlessly — they had to find the "false segment"), and the Choir of Shadows (echoes of dead Sovereign-worshippers)
  • Hours IV-V: Discovery of the Frozen Moments — temporal prisons where Fursen was held
  • Hour XI: The Frozen City — a city suspended in time. The party encountered a bookkeeper who offered to trade memories for passage. Sol traded a personal memory for Clockmaker's Touch — a temporal manipulation ability.
  • Hour XII: The Critical Hour — the trial attempted to erase Kael as an "ontological anomaly." It proved he "should not exist" and tried to erase him from reality. Kael survived despite being declared impossible.
  • Hour XIII: Expulsion — the trial declared the party "metaphysical errors" and violently expelled them

What Happened to Each Party Member

  • Sol Raven: In the Frozen City (Hour XI), traded a personal memory to the bookkeeper for Clockmaker's Touch — a temporal manipulation ability that deepened his connection to pre-Sovereign power
  • Jonas: Attempted to intimidate Imhuran directly. Received a temporal brand on his chest as punishment — spreading corruption that was later cleansed by Mira at the Pallid Crossroads
  • Kael: Hour XII proved he is an ontological anomaly — absent from Imhuran's temporal ledger. Revealed as a fragment vomited by Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress. A being with no origin point who exists despite never being born.
  • Fursen: Found imprisoned in temporal stasis — frozen outside of time, aware but unable to move for what may have been centuries. The party freed him during Hours IV-V. Fursen had served his sentence — the party simply collected him upon release.

Significance

The trial attempted to break them but failed. They survived divine judgment that should have destroyed them and were declared "certified anomalies that reality itself cannot properly process."

items.title: The Blooming Rot
items.content:
The Blooming Rot

Domain: Talaris Bloomrend the Verdant King | Type: Living Forest

Overview

The Blooming Rot is not a forest — it IS Talaris Bloomrend. Every tree, every fungus, every blade of grass is connected through his consciousness. The forest watches because the Verdant King watches. It remembers because he remembers.

Death feeds life here. Corpses bloom into flowers. Decay becomes growth. The cycle never stops.

The Pilgrim's Path (Sessions 18-19)

1. Sporehaven — Entry Point

A pilgrim waystation of fungal huts and moss-draped shelters clustered around a massive dead tree called the Pilgrim's Rest. Interior lit by bioluminescent fungi. Air smells of damp earth and cooking spores.

Residents are "Spore-Touched" — pilgrims who chose willing transformation into the cycle. Bark creeps up their skin, moss grows in their hair, their eyes glow with green luminescence.

What the party learned: The Verdant King has been "tasting their footsteps since the border." He sensed the preserved death (Mira's bones) they carried. Foreign Sovereign marks "stand out like a stone in compost." Honesty is mandatory — the King detects lies.

2. The Sweetwater Marsh

A low-lying wetland where the forest floor gives way to black water and tussock islands. The water is crystal clear — deceptively so. It's filtered through fungal root systems but carries addictive properties.

Hazard: Sweetwater Compulsion. Those who drink become charmed and compelled to stay. After 24 hours, the compulsion strengthens. After 48 hours, victims begin to calcify. The "Dreaming Stones" visible throughout the marsh are pilgrims who drank and never left — calcified, overgrown, still breathing.

3. The Pilgrim's Spine

A long, winding ridge of compacted earth and ancient root systems rising above the forest floor. The safest route through mid-forest — elevated above the worst predators, marked with fungal lanterns that glow at night.

Hazard: Narrow path. Some sections require careful navigation. The drop is 30-60 feet into dense undergrowth. Things move in the shadow below, looking up at the lit path, waiting for someone to fall.

4. The Hunger Garden

A vast basin where the forest is "wrong." The canopy is so thick no sunlight reaches the floor. Everything is lit by bioluminescence — beautiful and deadly. Plants have learned animals are food. Animals have learned plants are food. This is Talaris's digestive system at its most active.

Hazards: Natural darkness (only bioluminescence). Active predation — encounters every 30 minutes. Maw of the Garden traps (pit traps that digest). Gullet Vines. Corpse Lily Lurkers. Thornback Behemoths that everything avoids.

The Bone Trail — previous pilgrims' remains mark the "safe" path.

5. The Congregation Grove

A ring of thirteen massive trees (each over 200 feet tall) surrounding a clearing of soft moss and perpetual green twilight. Sacred site where Talaris listens. Air thick with spores that "think."

The Choir Blooms questioned the party:

  • "What do you carry into the King's domain?"
  • "What do you seek at the Wound?"
  • "Who sent you, and why?"
  • "What would you offer for passage?"

The party gave honest answers — they carried the bones of someone who wanted to reunite with her husband. Honest answers are rewarded. Lies are detected and punished.

6. Verdant Pass (The Wound)

A scar where the Blooming Rot simply stops. See separate entry.

Party History

The party traveled through the Blooming Rot carrying Mira's remains. They burned creatures twice with fire (angering the forest). But at the Congregation Grove, Sol shared a memory of Aedwynn the Maker with Talaris — the Verdant King saw his creator alive for the first time since the Usurpation. The Choir Blooms wept golden spores.

Talaris said: "She was... beautiful."

The fire is not forgiven. But the memory changed something.

It's filtered through fungal root systems but carries addictive properties. Hazard: Sweetwater Compulsion. Those who drink become charmed and compelled to stay. After 24 hours, the compulsion strengthens. After 48 hours, victims begin to calcify. The "Dreaming Stones" visible throughout the marsh are pilgrims who drank and never left — calcified, overgrown, still breathing. 3. The Pilgrim's Spine A long, winding ridge of compacted earth and ancient root systems rising above the forest floor. The safest route through mid-forest — elevated above the worst predators, marked with fungal lanterns that glow at night. Hazard: Narrow path. Some sections require careful navigation. The drop is 30-60 feet into dense undergrowth. Things move in the shadow below, looking up at the lit path, waiting for someone to fall. 4. The Hunger Garden A vast basin where the forest is "wrong." The canopy is so thick no sunlight reaches the floor. Everything is lit by bioluminescence — beautiful and deadly. Plants have learned animals are food. Animals have learned plants are food. This is Talaris's digestive system at its most active. Hazards: Natural darkness (only bioluminescence). Active predation — encounters every 30 minutes. Maw of the Garden traps (pit traps that digest). Gullet Vines. Corpse Lily Lurkers. Thornback Behemoths that everything avoids. The Bone Trail — previous pilgrims' remains mark the "safe" path. 5. The Congregation Grove A ring of thirteen massive trees (each over 200 feet tall) surrounding a clearing of soft moss and perpetual green twilight. Sacred site where Talaris listens. Air thick with spores that "think." The Choir Blooms questioned the party: "What do you carry into the King's domain?" "What do you seek at the Wound?" "Who sent you, and why?" "What would you offer for passage?" The party gave honest answers — they carried the bones of someone who wanted to reunite with her husband. Honest answers are rewarded. Lies are detected and punished. 6. Verdant Pass (The Wound) A scar where the Blooming Rot simply stops. See separate entry. Party History The party traveled through the Blooming Rot carrying Mira's remains. They burned creatures twice with fire (angering the forest). But at the Congregation Grove, Sol shared a memory of Aedwynn the Maker with Talaris — the Verdant King saw his creator alive for the first time since the Usurpation. The Choir Blooms wept golden spores. Talaris said: "She was... beautiful." The fire is not forgiven. But the memory changed something.

items.title: Verdant Pass
items.content:
Verdant Pass — The Wound

Location: Within the Blooming Rot | Type: Anomaly

Overview

Verdant Pass is a scar in the living forest — a narrow ravine where the Blooming Rot simply stops. Grey stone. Bare rock. No moss, no fungi, no life. The forest refuses to grow here.

Something cut Talaris when he claimed this territory. The cut healed around the resistance but never consumed it. The Verdant King cannot enter the Wound.

The Cave of Waiting

Within the pass lies a small cave where Elias Alethain crawled to die thirty years ago. He chose to die where Talaris couldn't claim his body — a final act of defiance against the Sovereigns.

The party found his skeleton there, untouched by decay. They laid Mira's remains beside him, placed her wedding ring on her finger, and positioned her hand to hold his.

She can finally rest with her beloved.

items.title: The Pallid Crossroads
items.content:
The Pallid Crossroads

Type: Neutral Settlement | Location: Ossathar (Vortegas the Bone-Warden's territory)

Overview

A neutral desert settlement located on the Spine Continent, sitting at the crossroads of major trade routes. The air carries sound strangely here — whispers travel far, shouts die quickly. Perhaps a dozen permanent residents call it home.

This settlement sits within Vortegas's borders but operates independently — a waystation for travelers between domains.

Key Locations

  • The Dry Cup — Tavern and inn serving as the settlement's social center
  • Mira's Clinic — Where the healer "Mira Hemm" practiced for 22 years (now abandoned)
  • Threshold Outfitters — Run by Sera, whom Mira had been teaching basic medicine
  • The Stables — Where Fursen first encountered the Misremembered

Party History

Session 8 — Post-Trial Recovery

The party arrived after the 13-Hour Trial. Jonas bore Imhuran's temporal brand on his chest — spreading corruption causing him constant pain. They sought help and found Mira Hemm, a skilled healer.

Mira stabilized and cleansed Jonas's temporal corruption, removing the brand's power and saving him from slow temporal death. The party rested under her care.

Session 8A — The Misremembered

During the night, Fursen encountered the Misremembered in the stables — an entity that mimicked him perfectly but with wrong details. It left a message carved into wood: "TURN AROUND" and asked: "Where is the wrong thought?"

The Resonant Codex trembled in Kael's hands at the entity's presence. It was hunting for something specific — "not you" it told Fursen. Someone else.

Session 9 — The White Marcher

The party investigated a missing caravan east of Pallid Crossroads. They found overturned carts, drained corpses with blurred features and removed color. A White Marcher — a pale, faceless creature that consumed the idea of form — ambushed them. The party defeated it, and it collapsed into blank-white dust.

Sessions 10-11 — The Boundary Titan and the Collapse

The party witnessed a Boundary Titan striding across the horizon — impossibly tall, each step shaking the ground. They followed its trail northward and discovered its massive weight had collapsed the ground above an ancient buried structure.

Sessions 12-15 — The C-5 Facility (Terminus Ω-I)

Jonas descended through a service hatch into an abandoned maintenance level with a dead facility worker who had been there for centuries. He found evidence of a containment breach, C-5 access credentials, and a Null-Flesh sample.

The party unlocked the facility and descended deeper, discovering subunits for archives, biocell storage, power generation, and a lower archive core. When they restored power, they awakened Preservation Custodian C-5/Ω — an ancient mechanical guardian with four arms. They defeated it in brutal combat.

In the biocell storage, Jonas deliberately shattered a containment cylinder (twice), releasing a TYPE-09 specimen. The party defeated it but encountered another escaped specimen — TYPE-11 — in the narrow corridors. They activated Protocol Beta (a 3-minute purge countdown) and barely escaped before AGENT-TERMINUS saturated the facility.

This was proof that civilization existed before the Sovereigns — the facility contained bio-engineered organisms called Null-Flesh, predating divine rule.

Session 16 — The Misremembered and Vorath

Back at Pallid Crossroads, the settlement fell silent as mist formed an unmoving ring. The Misremembered appeared — a hunter sent by Karthayne to "correct" Sol, whom it called a "wrong thought" unrecordable because he's connected to Aedwynn. The party defeated it, but it left a warning: "NOT CORRECTED. NOTED. WILL RETURN."

Mira revealed her true identity as Mira Alethain — last of the Seven Heretics, hunted for 30 years. She explained Kael's nature: a fragment vomited by Vor'Kael, a paradox who exists despite never being born. Kael had an existential breakdown.

Mira asked the party to investigate Vorath at Dusthollow — a man asking about her. They discovered he was a Tribunal assassin with a list of the seven heretics (six crossed out). After confronting him, Mira killed Vorath with her fist. They interrogated his corpse and learned about Kaleth Vorn, Seraphine Vale, and the Tribunal's hunt. Mira joined the party for the journey to Veinspire.

Session 17 — The Underways and Kaleth Vorn

The party traveled five days through the underground: the Cistern Depths (flooded tunnels, blind crawlers), the Bone Galleries (ancient catacombs, Grave Wardens), and the Arterial Tunnels (Mareatha's domain, blood-drinkers).

They rescued Vela Thorne, a junior archivist who had discovered Kaleth's kill manifest. In the Ossuary Chamber, Kaleth activated a Sanguine Siphon to drain Mira's power — but her true power, Undying Conviction, was entirely her own. She rose wreathed in orange-gold fire and killed Kaleth in four strikes. His last words were never spoken.

Significance

The Pallid Crossroads was Mira's refuge for 22 years after her husband Elias died. She could never return after the party's visit — too many knew she was there. The settlement remains, but Mira's clinic stands empty.

items.title: Dusthollow
items.content:
Dusthollow

Type: Settlement | Status: Neutral Territory

Overview

A small waystation located half a day west of the Pallid Crossroads. Contains the Dry Cup tavern, stables, and a handful of residences. Perhaps a dozen permanent residents. Neutral ground used as a staging point for travelers.

The Vorath Encounter (Session 17)

The party learned that Vorath — a Tribunal agent hunting Mira — was camped at Dusthollow. They investigated his room at the Dry Cup and found:

  • Evidence connecting him to the Tribunal and Seraphine Vale
  • A sealed letter from "K" (Kaleth Vorn) with orders to capture Mira alive
  • Instructions to interrogate her about the Arterial Texts

The Kill

The party rode back through the night to catch Vorath before he could flee. They arrived before dawn to find him at the stables, preparing to escape on horseback.

Jonas threw his hammer (missed, hit the horse instead). Vorath was thrown from the saddle and surrendered, offering information for his life.

Mira bashed his head in with her fist. No hesitation.

Intelligence from Speak with Dead

The party interrogated Vorath's corpse:

  • "Who is K?" — "Kaleth Vorn. Tribunal Hand. Veinspire Catacomb. Seraphine's superior — gives her orders."
  • "The Arterial Texts?" — "The Vein-Mother's mortal writings. Her doubts. Her failures. Words before divinity. The Tribunal must destroy them. Alethain knows where they are hidden."
  • "State of Veinspire?" — "Theorists scattered. Three cells discovered. Executions public. The Church fears what stirs beneath. The Mother's heart beats... irregular."

Significance

This was the moment Mira permanently left her old life behind. After killing Vorath, she could never return to the Pallid Crossroads without endangering the settlement. From here, there was only forward — toward Veinspire, toward vengeance, toward death.

items.title: Lastwell
items.content:
Lastwell

Domain: Border of the Devoured Courts | Type: Border Settlement

Overview

Lastwell is the last settlement before entering Vor'Kael's domain proper. It sits at the edge where meaning begins to fade — colors become muted, words come harder, and concepts lose their edges.

Key Locations

  • The Husk Pen — Contains identity-stealing empty people called "husks." If you give them a name, they try to steal that identity.
  • The Last Hearth Tavern — Run by Marra, one of the few places to rest before the descent.
  • The Smith — A craftsman who sold Jonas the Closed Locket containing Vor'Kael's true name.
  • The Greeter — A man who warns travelers about the domain ahead. He has lost his own name.

Party History (Session 20)

The party stopped in Lastwell before entering Vor'Kael's domain. Fursen betrayed Jonas by whispering "Sir Jonas" to the husks, causing them to try to steal his identity. Jonas retaliated by throwing the Bleeding Blade into Fursen's thigh.

At the smith's shop, Jonas purchased a Closed Locket that responded to Azerach's brand on his hand. Through the brand, Azerach opened the locket and revealed Vor'Kael's true name — a secret he had kept for 800 years.

The meaning-dealer Sekris approached Fursen after the conflict and gave him a contract-token — a way to summon Sekris if Fursen wants something "removed."

items.title: The Devoured Courts
items.content:
The Devoured Courts

Domain: Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress | Type: Sovereign Domain

Overview

The Devoured Courts are Vor'Kael's domain — a realm where meaning itself is consumed. Colors fade to grey. Words become difficult. Concepts lose their substance. The deeper you go, the less things mean.

The Capital: Consumption

The border city of the Devoured Courts. Streets of grey, buildings that were once meaningful now standing as hollow shells. The party passed through on their way to the Mouth of the World.

The Mouth of the World

A vast depression where Vor'Kael waits — the entrance to the Throat of Forgotten Things. At this place:

  • Fursen's 10,000 Leonin ancestors screamed in recognition
  • The Resonant Codex (containing Kael's Echo) tried to throw itself into the depths
  • Jonas caught the Codex one inch from the edge, saving Kael's Echo
  • Vor'Kael spoke directly to the party, recognizing the names, the echo, and "a secret her brother keeps"

Party History (Session 20)

The party traveled through the Devoured Courts to reach the Throat of Forgotten Things. They passed through Consumption and descended into the Mouth of the World, where Vor'Kael acknowledged them before they entered her esophagus.

items.title: The Throat of Forgotten Things
items.content:
The Throat of Forgotten Things

Location: Inside Vor'Kael's Body | Type: 5-Day Passage

Overview

The Throat of Forgotten Things is a passage through Vor'Kael's esophagus — a 5-day journey through the body of a Sovereign. Flesh-walls, bile pools, and the remnants of everything she has consumed over 800 years.

Day 1: The Gullet

The entry passage — wet flesh 20 feet wide, 15 feet high, sloping downward. Constant bile pools (acidic, grey-green). The party found a skeleton in one of the pools — a woman who almost escaped 800 years ago, dissolving inches from freedom. Sol reached into the acid to retrieve her Remembrance Seal.

Day 2: The Lodged Kingdom

An entire civilization lodged in Vor'Kael's throat for 800 years. White stone architecture — arches, columns, domes — elegantly decayed. Population: 12 survivors called The Undigested.

The Undigested have pale grey-white translucent skin, milky faded eyes, and wear Remembrance Seals. They cannot leave — their Seals protect them in the Throat but fail in the outside world. They offered knowledge in exchange for freedom using Vor'Kael's name. Jonas refused to help them.

Day 3: The Concept Graveyard

A vast cavern filled with half-digested concepts given physical form:

  • A faceless woman (Longing)
  • A black liquid pool (Regret)
  • A skeleton with wrong bones (Madness/Joy)
  • A glass tree (Nostalgia)
  • A cracked mirror (Emptiness)

Here stood the Door of Possibility — standing alone, closed, something on the other side. Sol opened it and saw every version of himself he could have been. Fursen pulled him back, but the Possibilities poured in. Sol is now infected with unlived lives.

Day 4: The Satiated One

An obstruction chamber containing the Satiated One — an ancient serpentine entity older than the Sovereigns, perfectly content, immune to meaning-consumption because it has no meaning to consume.

The Satiated One has been lodged in Vor'Kael's throat for 800 years. It let the party pass after Jonas spoke Vor'Kael's true name — the first interesting thing in centuries. It warned Jonas that Vor'Kael will never forgive him for speaking the name, and spoke to Fursen about the choice between vengeance and stillness.

In its presence, Sol's Possibility whispers fell silent, and Fursen's 10,000 ancestors went quiet.

Day 5: Exit

The passage rises and steepens. Flesh-walls thin to translucent. The party emerged onto an empty road across the Maker's corpse, 2 days from Veinspire.

info.description: Allies, enemies, and everyone in between

items.title: Thessaly
items.content:
Thessaly

Role: Bookseller (official) / Dealer in Forbidden Texts (actual) | Location: The Frayed Codex, Lesser Sacral District

Who She Is

Thessaly is impossibly old. Her exact age is unknown, but she called Mira "girl" despite Mira being over 2,000 years old. Her skin is like crumpled parchment, her hair like white cobwebs, her fingers like bundled twigs. She wears spectacles with magnifying lenses and moves through her cramped shop with surprising certainty.

Officially, the Frayed Codex sells hymnals and devotional texts. Actually, Thessaly deals in restricted texts, pre-Usurpation fragments, and forbidden knowledge.

Connection to the Party

When the party visited in Session 18:

  • Recognized Mira immediately from 30 years ago when Mira fled Veinspire — "Thirty years, girl. Give or take."
  • Identified the Resonant Codex Kael carried — "That book is a Resonant Codex. There are perhaps four in existence. They don't get 'found' — they choose."
  • Sol read two books — The Doctrine of Still Air (Azerach's philosophy of silence) and a book on Vor'Kael that has a mind of its own — but returned them. Too expensive.
  • Gave Fursen The Ledger of Moments for FREE after Fursen begged for it — with a promise that he would return and tell her how his quest concludes

About the Leonin

When Thessaly saw Fursen, she was stunned:

"Leonin. An actual Leonin. Your kind has been gone for... how long? A thousand years? Two thousand? I've read about your people my entire life. Collected every fragment, every rumor, every half-burned document the Church tried to destroy. I never thought I'd see one."

Warning

"Reading them changes you — not always for the worse, but always permanently."

items.title: Mira Alethain
items.content:
Mira Alethain

Role: Former Companion | Status: Laid to Rest (Session 19)

Who She Was

Mira Alethain was the last of the Seven Heretics — a group of believers who were once devoted to Mareatha before they discovered the Arterial Texts thirty years ago. The texts revealed truths that shattered their faith. She was a Sanctified Champion, a holy warrior of immense power.

Her Quest

Mira's husband Elias traveled to the Verdant King's domain thirty years ago to retrieve herbs, but he never returned. Mira spent thirty years wondering what happened to him, searching for answers.

She found the party after they emerged from the Clockwork Sanctum and traveled with them, waiting for the chance to reach Verdant Pass.

Her End

In the arterial tunnels beneath Veinspire (Session 18), Mira triggered Undying Conviction to destroy Kaleth Vorn — burning her remaining lifeforce as divine fire. It worked. Kaleth burned.

But she was weakened, and Savaros consumed her in the Catacomb Reliquary before she could recover.

Laid to Rest

The party honored her final wish. They carried her remains through the Blooming Rot to Verdant Pass, found the Cave of Waiting where Elias's skeleton lay, and placed her beside him. Sol put her wedding ring on her finger and positioned her hand to hold her husband's.

She can finally rest with her beloved.

items.title: Garrett the Smith
items.content:
Garrett the Smith

Role: Discreet Craftsman | Location: The Whetstone and Anvil, Lesser Sacral District

Who He Is

Garrett is a mid-fifties smith with thick arms, burn scars, and hands that have shaped steel for 32 years. He doesn't ask questions and he doesn't talk to the Church. Seraphine directed the party to him because he could be trusted.

Services

Garrett provides equipment repair, assessment of unusual materials, and has pre-Sovereign gear available (expensive). When he assessed Jonas's Custodian Armplate, he noted the metal takes heat slowly — his forge isn't hot enough to shape it properly. Would need divine-fire heat or equivalent.

Legends He Shared

  • The Martyr's Gauntlet — Supposed to be in the Grand Cathedral vault, but the pedestal is empty. Has been for decades or centuries. Protects from lies and illusions.
  • The Bleeding Blade — Deep in the catacombs, below Tribunal operations. Made from crystallized divine blood. Wounds never stop bleeding. The area is unmapped; people don't return.
  • The Underways Warden Armor — Pre-Sovereign full plate. Garrett saw it standing empty in the tunnels once. When he returned a week later, it was gone. "Like it walked away on its own."
  • The Silenced Knight's Armor — Rumors speak of a knight who supposedly killed a Sovereign permanently. The knight was imprisoned, and the armor — which absorbed divine power — is sealed in the Tribunal's deepest prison.

items.title: Seraphine Vale
items.content:
Seraphine Vale

Role: Former High Inquisitor of the Mareathan Inquisition | Status: Unknown (post-Confluence)

Who She Is

Seraphine Vale was a High Inquisitor — one of Mareatha's elite hunters who tracked down heretics. She is 34 years old, stationed at the Church of the Merciful Vein in the Lesser Sacral District.

What she didn't know: Mira Alethain delivered her at birth and blessed her in Mareatha's name. Seraphine's entire life was shaped by doctrine that Mira once believed too.

The Confrontation (Session 18)

When the party reached the Church of the Merciful Vein, Seraphine had orders from Kaleth Vorn to capture them all. But Mira revealed the truth:

  • "Seraphine. Seraphine Vale. I delivered you. Thirty-four years ago..."
  • "I blessed you in Mareatha's name. I believed it then. I believed everything they told me, just like you do now."

The party showed her Kaleth's journal — evidence of his murders spanning thirty years, a kill list of six crossed-out names (the other six of the Seven Heretics), and his admission that he didn't believe any of the doctrine.

Her Choice

Seraphine's faith cracked. She dismissed her guards with a false report, kenneled the Blood-Hound, set aside the Obedience Draught she was supposed to use on Mira, and provided intelligence on Savaros, the Cardinal, and the Confluence.

Sol gave her Oathkeeper — Mira's legendary greatsword — as a gesture of trust.

Current Status

Unknown. Seraphine remained in Veinspire while the party traveled to lay Mira to rest. The Confluence occurred while they were away. Whatever happened in that ritual, Seraphine was in the city when it happened. The party doesn't know if she survived, if her cover held, or what side she's on now.

items.title: The Cardinal
items.content:
The Cardinal

Role: The Tribunal's Tribunal — Ultimate Authority | Status: Unknown

What Is Known

The Cardinal sits at the apex of Tribunal hierarchy. Every major decision passes through them — executions, doctrinal revisions, purges. They ordered the hunt for the Seven Heretics 30 years ago.

Seraphine's warning: "I don't know who the Cardinal is. No one does. Not even Savaros, I think... The Cardinal is a web. You won't see the threads until you're already tangled."

True Nature

The Cardinal's true nature is unknown. They operate through connections and consequences rather than direct action. Their identity is completely hidden.

The Confluence

The Confluence is a ritual connected to the Monastery of Still Blood (a location beneath Mareatha's Heart, outside Veinspire). The ritual was scheduled for the new moon. The Cardinal was planning to do something during the Confluence.

While the party was laying Mira to rest in Verdant Pass, the Confluence occurred.

The party has no information about what happened. Seraphine was in the city when it happened — her fate is unknown.

Intelligence Gap

Savaros's Ledger contains information about the Cardinal and the Confluence. The party has the Ledger but hasn't fully studied it yet.

items.title: Savaros the High Keeper
items.content:
Savaros the High Keeper

Role: Ancient Skin-Walker serving Ultharion | Status: Destroyed (Session 18)

What He Was

Savaros was one of Ultharion's Skin-Walkers. He killed the previous High Keeper and stole his identity — the party found the previous Keeper's body lying on a bed in the Reliquary, which Jonas later burned.

Appearance

Robed figure in arterial-spray crimson, wearing an ornate mask depicting Mareatha's weeping face.

His voice was like paper tearing — dozens of layered voices slightly out of sync. Different accents and tones bled through from stolen identities. He could no longer speak normally.

Mirror vulnerability: Mirrors revealed his void form. He had removed all mirrors from the Catacomb Reliquary.

His End (Session 18)

In the Catacomb Reliquary, Savaros revealed his true nature. He consumed Mira Alethain — ripped her flesh from bone. Then he consumed Kael.

But Kael's impossible nature — the paradox of a being who was never born, a fragment that Vor'Kael the Hollow Empress couldn't digest — destroyed Savaros from within. The Skin-Walker couldn't process something with no origin.

His body convulsed as stolen identities screamed. His robes collapsed around emptiness.

Aftermath

The party found Kael's skeleton beside the Resonant Codex, which had transformed to contain an echo of who he was.

They recovered Savaros's Ledger — Tribunal intelligence, including details about the Cardinal, the Confluence, and the Monastery of Still Blood.

items.title: Kaleth Vorn
items.content:
Kaleth Vorn

Role: Tribunal Hand — Chief Assassin/Interrogator | Status: Deceased (Session 17)

What He Was

Kaleth Vorn was the Tribunal's blade in shadow — their chief assassin and interrogator. He worked for Savaros ("S"), who himself reported to the Cardinal. For 30 years, he hunted the Seven Heretics with methodical patience.

Seraphine Vale admired him. She didn't know he was using her as a pawn, or that he was a cynical murderer who didn't believe any of the doctrine he enforced.

The Kill Manifest

His journal contained a list of the Seven Heretics — six names crossed out:

  • Aldric Sarne — poison, Year 3
  • Elias Alethain — Savaros pushed him from cliff, Year 8 (Mira's husband)
  • Corwin Marsh — blade, Year 12
  • Daven Holt — hired bandits, Year 17
  • Bram Ashford — arranged duel, Year 21
  • Adelaide Dunne — official execution, Year 26
  • Mira Alethain — (attempted, Session 17 — FAILED)

He kept trophies: six rings on a chain — one from each heretic he killed.

His End (Session 18)

The party confronted him in the arterial tunnels beneath Veinspire. Mira triggered Undying Conviction — burning her remaining lifeforce as divine fire.

Four strikes in less than four seconds. His last words were never spoken — Mira didn't let him finish his monologue. Kaleth burned.

Intelligence Found

On his body:

  • Sealed letter from "S" (Savaros) mentioning "The Cardinal" and "The Confluence"
  • Journal with cynical admissions: "Sometimes I wonder if any of them actually believe. I stopped believing decades ago."
  • Kill manifest of the Seven Heretics
  • Brass key with Pre-Usurpation symbol

items.title: Vela Thorne
items.content:
Vela Thorne

Role: Former Junior Archivist, Mareathan Church | Status: Active — Traveling with Party

Who She Is

Vela Thorne was a junior archivist in the Mareathan Church, approximately 30 years old. She worked in the archives where forbidden documents were stored — including evidence of the Church's crimes.

She found Kaleth Vorn's manifest of "resolved" heretics. For this discovery, she was captured by the Tribunal and tortured as bait for Mira.

Rescue (Session 17)

The party found Vela held by Tribunal guards, being used to lure Mira into a trap. They rescued her and brought her with them.

Expertise

Vela knows things the party needs:

  • Pre-Usurpation history
  • Catacomb layouts
  • Leonin history (fragments)
  • Church secrets and operations
  • Archive locations and contents

Current State

Traumatized but functional. She flinches at sudden movements. She doesn't sleep well. But she's determined to see the Church's lies exposed.

She travels with the party as a guide and information source — someone who knows how the Tribunal operates from the inside.

items.title: Sekris
items.content:
Sekris

Role: Meaning-Dealer | Location: Lastwell (border of Vor'Kael's domain)

Who He Is

Sekris is a meaning-dealer — someone who arranges for meanings to be "removed." In a land where meaning itself can be consumed, there are those who broker in its deliberate destruction. Sekris is one of them.

He appeared in Lastwell after Jonas stabbed Fursen, approaching the wounded Leonin with an offer.

The Contract-Token

Sekris gave Fursen a smoke-grey coin — a contract-token. Breaking the coin summons Sekris to arrange for something to be "removed." The implication was clear: Fursen could arrange for Jonas's death, or for the meaning of their conflict to simply... disappear.

The deal is not yet made. Fursen holds the token but hasn't broken it.

Nature

Sekris operates in the space between Vor'Kael's domain and the outside world — where meaning thins but hasn't vanished entirely. He profits from those who want things unmade, memories erased, or existences ended without the messiness of conventional violence.

His exact nature is unknown. He may be a servant of Vor'Kael, an independent operator, or something else entirely.

items.title: The Usurpation
items.content:
The Usurpation

Type: Historical Event — The Murder of Aedwynn the Maker

What Happened

Before the current age, there was one god: Aedwynn the Maker. She created the world, shaped life, and maintained reality. Thirteen of her most trusted servants conspired to kill her.

They murdered Aedwynn and divided her body among themselves. Each claimed an organ, a piece of divine power. They became the Thirteen Sovereigns — gods built from the corpse of their creator.

  • Mareatha took the Heart
  • Azerach took the Ears
  • Imhuran took the Eyes
  • Talaris took the Liver
  • Vor'Kael took the Stomach
  • Eredain took the Voice
  • Karthayne took the Heart of Creation
  • Ultharion took the Skin
  • ...and five others claimed the remaining organs

The World Now

The Sovereigns rule their domains absolutely. Each has reshaped their territory according to their nature. Their cults enforce worship. Their agents hunt those who remember what came before.

But fragments of Aedwynn survived. Divine shards embedded in mortals. Echoes of the Maker's power hiding in ancient places. And now Sol Raven — the Maker's Witness — carries her memory forward.

items.title: Aedwynn the Maker
items.content:
Aedwynn the Maker

Status: Deceased (Murdered during the Usurpation)

Who She Was

Aedwynn the Maker was the god who created the world. She shaped reality, breathed life into existence, and maintained the balance of all things. She was not distant or unknowable — she walked among her creations, crafting and caring for the world she made.

Thirteen of her trusted servants betrayed her. They killed her and divided her body among themselves, becoming the Sovereigns who rule the world today.

Her Legacy

Aedwynn is not entirely gone. Fragments of her divine essence survived:

  • Her preserved body lies in the Chamber of the Final Breath, discovered by the party in Session 18
  • Divine shards embedded themselves in certain mortals (the Aedwynn-Born)
  • Sol Raven carries one such shard, animating his skeletal form
  • Ancient facilities built by the Maker still respond to those who carry her essence

The Witness

In the Chamber of the Final Breath, the party found a white-barked tree with veins of gold rising from the stone. At its base sat Aedwynn's preserved body — a shell containing an echo of her consciousness, uploaded onto this puppet made from the tree. When the party touched her, she awakened. She recognized Sol as carrying a piece of herself, and gave him the role of Witness — her heir, carrying her memory forward into an age ruled by her murderers.

Sol can now access complete memories of the world before the Usurpation. He showed one such memory to Talaris Bloomrend — the first time the Verdant King had seen his creator since helping to kill her.

items.title: The Resonant Codex
items.content:
The Resonant Codex

Type: Artifact | Current Bearer: Sol Raven

What It Is

The Resonant Codex is a book that records connections — the threads that bind the Sovereigns to the Maker's stolen power, the echoes of divine essence, the patterns that underlie reality.

It was originally a tool for studying the relationship between the Sovereigns and Aedwynn's fragmented divinity.

Kael's Echo

When Kael died in the Catacomb Reliquary, the Codex transformed. The party found it beside his skeleton, its pages changed. It now contains an echo of Kael — not his soul exactly, but an imprint of who he was.

Sol can access this echo, consulting the memory of his friend who was never born but chose to exist anyway.

items.title: The Bleeding Blade
items.content:
The Bleeding Blade

Type: Cursed Longsword | Bearer: Jonas

The Curse

The Bleeding Blade is a longsword that hungers for blood. It must be fed daily — blood from combat, from sacrifice, from any source. If Jonas fails to feed it, the blade turns on him, drinking from its bearer instead.

The sword whispers to him sometimes. He's learned to manage the curse, but managing isn't the same as controlling.

Origin

Jonas claimed the blade from the Altar after the party killed Savaros in the Catacomb Reliquary. Whether the sword was always cursed or became so is unclear.

Current Status

The blade needs feeding tomorrow. It always needs feeding tomorrow.

items.title: Vor'Kael's True Name
items.content:
Vor'Kael's True Name

Type: Ultimate Leverage | Bearer: Jonas

What It Is

Vor'Kael's true name — the name she had before the Usurpation, before she became the Hollow Empress, before she claimed the Maker's Stomach. A secret that Azerach the Whispered King kept hidden for 800 years.

How It Was Found

In Lastwell, Jonas purchased a Closed Locket from a smith who didn't know what he had. The locket was sealed to those who bore Azerach's brand. When Jonas touched it, the brand on his hand flared cold and the locket opened, revealing Vor'Kael's name etched inside.

Azerach had kept this secret for eight centuries — leverage against a fellow Sovereign. Now Jonas holds it.

The Cost

Jonas spoke the name aloud twice in Vor'Kael's domain — once to test it, once to command the Satiated One's passage. The Hollow Empress felt her true name spoken. She cannot unhear it.

Vor'Kael will hunt Jonas forever.

The Power

A Sovereign's true name grants power over them. The exact nature of this power is unclear, but it is enough that Azerach kept it secret for 800 years, and enough that the Satiated One — an entity older than the Sovereigns — recognized its significance.

A secret that Azerach the Whispered King kept hidden for 800 years. How It Was Found In Lastwell, Jonas purchased a Closed Locket from a smith who didn't know what he had. The locket was sealed to those who bore Azerach's brand. When Jonas touched it, the brand on his hand flared cold and the locket opened, revealing Vor'Kael's name etched inside. Azerach had kept this secret for eight centuries — leverage against a fellow Sovereign. Now Jonas holds it. The Cost Jonas spoke the name aloud twice in Vor'Kael's domain — once to test it, once to command the Satiated One's passage. The Hollow Empress felt her true name spoken. She cannot unhear it. Vor'Kael will hunt Jonas forever. The Power A Sovereign's true name grants power over them. The exact nature of this power is unclear, but it is enough that Azerach kept it secret for 800 years, and enough that the Satiated One — an entity older than the Sovereigns — recognized its significance.

items.title: The Remembrance Seal
items.content:
The Remembrance Seal

Type: Protective Artifact | Bearer: Sol Raven

What It Is

A pendant of black iron, cold to the touch. It grants its bearer advantage on saving throws against memory alteration effects. Inside the seal is a preserved memory — the final memory of its previous owner.

How It Was Found

In the bile pools of Vor'Kael's Throat, Sol found a skeleton — a woman who had almost escaped the Hollow Empress's domain 800 years ago. She was dissolving inches from freedom when she died. Around her neck was the Remembrance Seal.

Sol retrieved it from her remains and now carries her memory forward.

The Final Memory

The seal contains the woman's last moments — her desperate crawl through the bile, the burning as her flesh dissolved, the sight of freedom just out of reach. She wore the seal to remember who she was as Vor'Kael tried to consume her meaning. She almost made it.

items.title: Campaign Summary
items.content:
Campaign Summary

Current Session: 20 | Party Level: 10 | Location: Empty road, 2 days from Veinspire

The Story So Far

Sessions 0-4: Veinspire

Jonas and Sol were hired as escorts to Veinspire. They explored the city, descended into corrupted catacombs, witnessed dark rituals, and fled when the Tribunal closed in.

Session 5: The Choir Priest

The party stole a hymn fragment and was confronted by a Choir Priest. The battle ended with the priest's death, a collapsing church, and Sol's first ascension in a floating village.

Sessions 6-7: The Silent Dominion

The party traveled through Azerach's domain, learning to guard their words and thoughts. Jonas was branded by the Whispered King.

Sessions 7-8: The 13-Hour Trial

In Imhuran's Clockwork Sanctum, the party underwent a trial of temporal worth. Sol traded a memory for power, Kael's paradox nature was revealed, and Fursen was freed.

Sessions 9-15: The C-5 Facility

The party defeated a White Marcher, tracked a Boundary Titan, and discovered the pre-Sovereign C-5 facility. They defeated the Preservation Custodian, encountered bio-engineered Null-Flesh specimens, and escaped via Protocol Beta.

Sessions 16-17: Mira Joins and the Underways

The party defeated the Misremembered (Karthayne's hunter), and Mira revealed her true identity. After killing the Tribunal assassin Vorath, Mira joined them. They traveled through the Underways and killed Kaleth Vorn with Undying Conviction.

Session 18: The Maker's Witness and Catacomb Reliquary

The party discovered the Chamber of the Final Breath — a white-barked tree with veins of gold, at its base Aedwynn's preserved body containing an echo of her consciousness. When they touched her, she awakened and recognized Sol. He became the Maker's Witness. In the Catacomb Reliquary, Savaros consumed both Mira and Kael. Kael destroyed Savaros from within. Both died.

Session 19: Mira's Rest

Fursen carried Mira's remains in a barrel through the Blooming Rot to Verdant Pass. Sol showed Talaris a vision of Aedwynn. Mira was laid beside Elias in the Cave of Waiting.

Session 20: The Throat of Forgotten Things

The party traveled through Lastwell, a border town where meaning fades. Fursen betrayed Jonas by giving his name to identity-stealing husks; Jonas retaliated by throwing the Bleeding Blade into Fursen's thigh. At the smith's shop, Jonas acquired a Closed Locket containing Vor'Kael's true name — a secret Azerach had kept for 800 years.

Through the Devoured Courts, the party descended into the Throat of Forgotten Things — a 5-day journey through Vor'Kael's esophagus. They found a skeleton in the bile pools (Sol retrieved the Remembrance Seal), discovered the Lodged Kingdom (12 survivors trapped for 800 years), and traversed the Concept Graveyard where Sol opened the Door of Possibility and became infected with unlived lives.

In the depths, they encountered the Satiated One — an ancient entity older than the Sovereigns, perfectly content, immune to meaning-consumption. It let them pass after Jonas spoke Vor'Kael's true name. Jonas and Fursen reconciled (medical kit and 100 gold). The party emerged onto an empty road, 2 days from Veinspire.

Current Situation

  • Location: Empty road across the Maker's corpse, 2 days from Veinspire
  • Jonas: Holds Vor'Kael's true name — she will hunt him forever for speaking it aloud
  • Sol: Infected with Possibility — disadvantage on WIS saves, whispers of unlived lives
  • Fursen: Holds Sekris's contract-token — could summon a meaning-dealer to arrange for something to be "removed"
  • Concern: The Confluence happened in Veinspire while the party was in the Throat — unknown consequences
  • Threat: Vor'Kael will hunt Jonas forever for speaking her true name

Creatures Encountered

The party has faced various servants and horrors of the Sovereigns:

  • Blood Beasts - Vascular constructs serving Mareatha in Veinspire
  • The White Marcher - A solitary entity that "cleans" the Pallid Crossroads
  • Gravewardens - Sentinels guarding the catacombs
  • Choir Priest - A servant of Eredain wielding gravity-hymns (killed in Session 5)
  • Preservation Custodian C-5/Ω - Ancient mechanical guardian from the pre-Sovereign facility (destroyed)
  • The Misremembered - Karthayne's reality-erasing hunters (encountered, retreated)
  • Shape-Eaters - Creatures in the Blooming Rot that consume and become their prey
  • Savaros the High Keeper - A Skin-Walker serving Ultharion (destroyed by Kael)
  • The Husks - Empty people in Lastwell who steal identities when given names
  • The Undigested - 12 survivors trapped 800 years in Vor'Kael's throat
  • The Satiated One - Ancient serpentine entity, older than the Sovereigns, immune to meaning-consumption
